title: Pilosella vahlii
intro: Pilosella vahlii, also known as Vahl's mouse-ear, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating conditions such as arthritis and improving overall immune function. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella vahlii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Pilosella vahlii is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are employed in traditional medicine to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Pilosella vahlii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, which is a group of plants often referred to as "hawkweeds." The genus Pilosella is further divided into several species, with Pilosella vahlii being one of them. This plant is native to alpine regions in Europe and Asia. It is a low-growing, herbaceous perennial with yellow flowers.
